title: video.json — shots, representative frames, palettes, motion, captions
description: Written only for video inputs (audio-only tracks: manifest marks the stage not_applicable and this file is absent). Shots are a sorted, non-overlapping, gap-free partition of the video span on the unified audio timeline. Captions come from a pluggable backend; backend 'null' means captioning was intentionally skipped (offline default), which consumers must distinguish from an empty-but-real caption.

video_span.description: Extent of the video stream on the unified timeline (normally 0 .. duration).

name.description: 'null' (offline no-op) or 'anthropic'. Others may appear later; consumers tolerate unknown names.

model.description: Present for API backends, e.g. a pinned Claude model id.

prompt_version.description: Version tag of the captioning prompt; part of the caption cache key.

shots.description: Sorted by start_seconds; contiguous partition (shot[i].end_seconds == shot[i+1].start_seconds).

index.description: 0-based shot number; frames/shot_%04d filenames use index+1.

start_frame.description: Source-video frame index, for reference back into the original file.

representative_frames.description: 1 frame (midpoint) for shots < 4 s, 2 frames (1/3, 2/3) otherwise.

path.description: Relative to track dir, e.g. 'frames/shot_0004_f1.jpg'. JPEG, max 1280 px wide.

sha256.description: Hash of the JPEG bytes; keys the caption cache.

palette.description: Dominant colors, k-means (k=5) in CIELab over the representative frame(s), sorted by proportion descending. Fewer than 5 swatches only if the frame has fewer distinct clusters.

proportion.description: Fraction of sampled pixels in this cluster; proportions sum to ~1.0.

motion.description: Dense optical-flow magnitude (Farnebäck, 12 fps, 320 px pass), normalized by frame diagonal — dimensionless and comparable across videos. Rough scale: <0.002 static, 0.002–0.01 gentle movement, >0.03 fast action or camera whip. Degenerate case: a shot too short to contain any 12 fps sample pair (< ~1/6 s) carries no motion evidence and reports 0.0/0.0, indistinguishable from static by design.

caption.description: Absent when caption_backend.name is 'null'. When present, one caption per shot (frames' captions merged by the backend).

text.description: One-sentence literal description of what is on screen.

tags.description: Lowercase snake_case tags: subjects, setting, lighting, mood, camera (e.g. 'close_up', 'neon_lighting', 'crowd'). Open vocabulary, prompt-steered.
